checks.a_analysis: The content from the source document appears sequentially in the output without any instances of duplication. Each numbered agenda item and meeting note is present only once in the JSON structure.

The heading hierarchy in the JSON is logical and reflects the visual structure of the document. It starts with an H2 ('Education Committee Meeting') followed by an H3 ('Approved- Minutes'), and uses another H2 for the 'Agenda' section title. There are no skipped levels.

The attendee table on page 1 has been significantly corrupted. The source PDF contains three distinct attendee tables/sections (Committee Members, Guests, and Staff). The output incorrectly merges the side-by-side Committee and Guest sections into a single 6-column table but truncates it, including only two rows of names (missing 7 committee members and 1 guest). Furthermore, the entire 'Staff' table is completely omitted from the conversion result.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document on page 1 contains attendee lists for Committee Members (9 names), Guests (3 names), and Staff (11 names) in tabular formats.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON table (element index 6) merges Committee and Guest headers into one row and contains only two data rows (Jeryl Anderson and Chuck Greene). The entire Staff section is missing.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order in the converted output follows the logical flow of the original document, progressing from the header and attendees through the numbered agenda items to the adjournment notice.

The primary agenda list (items 1-11) is fragmented into three separate blocks because items 4 and 5 were converted to paragraphs to accommodate the intervening voting tables. This breaks the structural continuity of the single list. Additionally, the nested sub-list under item 10, which uses alphabetic markers (a, b, c) in the source, is rendered as an unordered list in the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda items 1-11 form a single continuous numbered list. Item 10 includes a sub-list with alphabetic markers (a, b, c).
checks.b_evidence_output: Agenda items 1-3 are in element index 8 (list), item 4 is a paragraph (index 9), item 5 is a paragraph (index 11), and items 6-11 are in a second list (index 13). The sub-list for item 10 is 'list_type': 'unordered'.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: The semantic JSON contains 3 headings (element indices 1, 2, and 7). The HTML metadata reports 5 headings. The mismatch of 2 headings is below the threshold of 3 defined in the evaluation rules (max of 10% of 3 or 3).
The source document contains no URLs or hyperlinks. The JSON output correctly reflects this by containing no links, preventing any link injection defects.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output significantly departs from the visual organization of the source PDF. Firstly, the attendee list section, which in the PDF is organized into distinct columns/tables for Committee Members, Guests, and Staff, is collapsed into a single table in the HTML and then severely truncated, missing the vast majority of participants. Secondly, the agenda's visual flow is broken: items 1-3 are in one list, but items 4 and 5 are rendered as plain paragraphs, and items 6-11 are placed in a new ordered list. This causes the browser to reset numbering (e.g., '6. Legislative Review' would render as '1.'), which contradicts the source's intent and numbering scheme.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF shows a complete list of 9 Committee Members, 3 Guests, and 11 Staff members, followed by an agenda numbered 1 through 11.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML attendee table only contains 2 rows of names and stops. The agenda numbering is split across multiple elements, with items 6-11 starting in a new <ol> block, which resets visual numbering in a browser.

checks.a_analysis: The primary table for 'Meeting Attendees' is visually corrupted through severe truncation. While the HTML uses a <table> structure, it only includes the first two rows of data. It omits seven Committee Members (Grayson Gusa, J.D. Hartman, Heather Joyner, Hope Downs, Brian Short, Jimmy Stewart, Donna Wright), one Guest (Ike Avery), and the entire Staff table (11 people). A user cannot read or understand the full attendee data as presented in the PDF.
checks.b_evidence_source: In the source PDF, page 1, there are tables/lists containing names for 9 Committee Members, 3 Guests, and 11 Staff members.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML, the 'Meeting Attendees' table only contains 2 rows: Jeryl Anderson and Chuck Greene. All subsequent rows and the entire staff section are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: First table in the document, following 'Meeting Attendees:'

checks.a_analysis: The NC DIT 911 Board logo is present in the HTML as a base64 encoded image and renders correctly. No other meaningful images are present in the source.
No visual duplication of content blocks (paragraphs, tables, or sections) was observed in the HTML output.

The overall visual quality is degraded due to the extreme truncation of the attendee list and the broken list numbering for the agenda. While the text that remains is readable, the document feels incomplete and incorrectly structured compared to the original minutes.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF is a cohesive 3-page document with complete attendee records and a continuous 11-item agenda.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML is an incomplete representation, missing significant portions of the 'Attendees' data and breaking the numbering of the 'Agenda' section.
